body_txt: Twins often argue, among themselves or with others, about the respect they are due because of age, even though the difference is minuscule. In the most common form, Alice will attempt to give her side leverage by saying that she is the oldest, which will cause Bob to roll his eyes and retort "Yeah, by seven minutes," and therefore defuse such an argument. If that fails to defuse it, it can easily spiral into an Argument of Contradictions due to its inherent pettiness. Even if the siblings don't argue over this, establishing the birth order of a set of twins, triplets, and so on also serves the narrative purpose of letting the story assign them the appropriate older/younger sibling tropes. The eldest might be the responsible (or just bossy) one, the middle one a Deadpan Snarker and/or overshadowed by their siblings, and the youngest the most youthful and exuberant. Overlaps with Playing the Age Card if the older one tries to use their (technically) greater age as a justification for bossing their (technically) younger sibling around. A common variant can involve an Older Nephew, Younger Uncle with the aunt/uncle and niece/nephew who are close in age arguing about who has authority over who.
